Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Understanding the Go Runtime: The Scheduler 109 upvotes, 11 comments
  2. Prefix sums at gigabytes per second with ARM NEON 37 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. The Met releases high-def 3D scans of 140 famous art objects 281 upvotes, 54 comments
  4. Executing programs inside transformers with exponentially faster inference 84 upvotes, 13 comments
  5. WolfIP: Lightweight TCP/IP stack with no dynamic memory allocations 127 upvotes, 23 comments
  6. IMG_0416 (2024) 88 upvotes, 17 comments
  7. Vite 8.0 Is Out 257 upvotes, 65 comments
  8. Bubble Sorted Amen Break 331 upvotes, 99 comments
  9. Malus – Clean Room as a Service 1256 upvotes, 455 comments
  10. Shall I implement it? No 1256 upvotes, 470 comments
  11. Willingness to look stupid 308 upvotes, 110 comments
  12. “This is not the computer for you” 431 upvotes, 167 comments
  13. Celebrating Interesting Flickr Technologies 33 upvotes, 7 comments
  14. Reversing memory loss via gut-brain communication 307 upvotes, 121 comments
  15. Document poisoning in RAG systems: How attackers corrupt AI's sources 118 upvotes, 46 comments
  16. Innocent woman jailed after being misidentified using AI facial recognition 604 upvotes, 309 comments
  17. Never Snooze a Future 13 upvotes, 2 comments
  18. Launch HN: IonRouter (YC W26) – High-throughput, low-cost inference 63 upvotes, 25 comments
  19. Bringing Chrome to ARM64 Linux Devices 105 upvotes, 47 comments
  20. Worldwide Sidewalk Joy: Adding whimsy to neighborhoods 15 upvotes, 3 comments
  21. Bucketsquatting Is (Finally) Dead 27 upvotes, 10 comments
  22. Big data on the cheapest MacBook 352 upvotes, 278 comments
  23. Hyperlinks in Terminal Emulators 54 upvotes, 36 comments
  24. Are LLM merge rates not getting better? 149 upvotes, 134 comments
  25. ATMs didn’t kill bank teller jobs, but the iPhone did 415 upvotes, 439 comments
  26. US private credit defaults hit record 9.2% in 2025, Fitch says 356 upvotes, 410 comments
  27. Ceno, browse the web without internet access 6 upvotes, 2 comments
  28. Specimen Gallery – CC0 transparent specimen PNGs organized by taxonomy 4 upvotes, 1 comments
  29. Can you instruct a robot to make a PBJ sandwich? 27 upvotes, 30 comments
  30. Grief and the AI split 143 upvotes, 220 comments

Last generated 5:30pm, 13 March 2026. Source here.
